data.title: Cloud storage service TeraBox introduces AI toolkit for content creation, enhancing productivity in cloud space. Launch in Tokyo.
data.description: A new set of AI productivity tools has been introduced by TeraBox, a leading cloud storage provider based in Tokyo. This toolkit, designed to streamline content creation in the cloud-native sector, enables users to generate content with just one click. The company unveiled this innovation on July 25, 2025, aiming to enhance efficiency and user experience. TeraBox's headquarters in Tokyo serves as the hub for these globally recognized cloud services, ensuring seamless operations for their wide customer base.

data.title: Shanton Pharma's SAP-001 for refractory gout program gets Fast Track Designation from US FDA.
data.description: Shanton Pharma, a clinical-stage biotech company, has disclosed that SAP-001, its main investigational compound, shows promise as a First- and Best-in-Class option for uncontrolled gout. The company, headquartered in Singapore and Princeton, N.J., made this statement on July 25, 2025. The FDA has recently acknowledged the development, emphasizing its potential significance in the treatment of gout.

data.title: Centralization crisis = threat to data privacy due to reliance on centralized cloud & IRS's proposed "mega API." Decentralized tech can help.
data.description: Dependence on centralized cloud infrastructure and the IRS's introduction of the proposed "mega API" highlight vulnerabilities in data privacy and accountability. Decentralized technologies offer solutions to these issues.

data.title: Lear Corporation's stock fell despite beating Q2 earnings, with global production up 3% and revenue at $6.03 billion.
data.description: Shares of Lear Corporation (NYSE: LEA) experienced a drop on Friday despite surpassing second-quarter earnings and revenue expectations, reporting revenue of $6.03 billion, which remained stable compared to the previous year. When excluding the effects of commodities, foreign exchange, tariff recoveries, and acquisitions and divestitures, sales saw a decrease of 1%. In the same period, global vehicle production saw a 3% increase year-over-year. Production in North America declined by 3%, in Europe by 2%, and in China, there was a rise of 9%. On a sales-weighted basis, global vehicle production remained unchanged for Lear Corporation. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TGE and AMTD secure consecutive victories against IP infringements, global fashion magazine owner.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Paris, New York, and Singapore, AMTD Group Inc. (referred to as "AMTD" or the "Group") and The Generation Essentials Group ("TGE"), a branch of AMTD Digital, have collaboratively revealed themselves as global proprietors of fashion magazines. This announcement encompasses an undisclosed number of magazines published under their ownership.

data.title: Phillips 66 beats Q2 earnings with $973 million profit, $33.52 billion revenue, outlines plans to meet 2027 targets.
data.description: Phillips 66 (PSX) had a strong second quarter, beating analysts' expectations with adjusted earnings of $973 million ($2.38 per share) compared to the estimated $1.69 per share. Quarterly revenue was also higher than projected at $33.52 billion versus the expected $32.53 billion. GAAP earnings stood at $877 million ($2.15 per share), including a $239 million charge for accelerated depreciation at the Los Angeles Refinery, set to close by year-end. This performance was a significant improvement from the previous quarter's loss of $368 million ($0.90 per share). Refining utilization saw... The Dow experienced a decline of over 300 points amidst Alphabet and Tesla earnings reports, leading to decreased investor sentiment, although the Fear Index remained in the 'Extreme Greed' zone. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Inventor from Santa Paula, Calif., creates PUP STOP, a portable solar-powered water dispenser for pets.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on July 25, 2025, an inventor from Santa Paula, California shared details about their new invention called the PUP STOP. The inventor expressed a desire to provide pets with constant access to fresh water without any hassle. The solar-powered PUP STOP is a portable device designed for this purpose, offering a convenient solution for ensuring pets are always hydrated.

data.title: Kellanova (NYSE: K) declares regular dividend of $0.58 per share for the third quarter, payable on September 15, 2025, to shareholders of record as of September 2, 2025.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, Chicago-based company Kellanova (NYSE: K) revealed a dividend declaration of $0.58 per share on its common stock. This dividend is scheduled for payment on September 15, 2025, to shareholders recorded as of September 2, 2025.

data.title: Developers believe AI tech will enhance crypto safety, as per smart contract auditors.
data.description: Recent studies have raised concerns about the security of cryptocurrencies, but according to sources in the smart contract development and auditing industry, the use of AI-assisted coding is actually enhancing security measures. Developers and auditors noted that this technological advancement is proving to be beneficial in improving the overall security of crypto assets.

data.title: Five Star Technology Solutions to host AI and Accessibility Forum at University of Indianapolis on Sept 22, 2025.
data.description: An event called the AI and Accessibility Forum will be hosted by Five Star Technology Solutions on September 22, 2025, at the University of Indianapolis. The company, with over 20 years of experience in K-12 edtech, is organizing the forum to bring together K-12 educators.
